mappings.rationaleAI: A core text on kinship systems, marriage customs, descent rules, and cultural institutions across societies (outline A and D).
Examines laws and customs regarding marriage, divorce, legitimacy, domestic violence, and children's rights (outline A.2).
Explores sexual behaviour customs and marriage norms across cultures, relevant to outline A.2 on mate selection and sexual behaviour.
Directly addresses varying marriage customs and practices across cultures and religions (outline A.2).
Traces historical changes in kinship, marriage, descent, and gendered social structures across cultures (outline A.6).
Addresses major social structures including class systems, stratification by status, and the relationship between marriage and society (outline B).
Examines class systems and status-based social organization including feudalism and hereditary privilege (outline B.2).
Slavery as a system of social organization characterized by unfree labor and serfdom is the central subject (outline B.2).
Traces the movement to dismantle slavery and serfdom as forms of social organization (outline B.2).
Explores economic systems including production, division of labour, exchange of goods, and property rights (outline C).
Examines capitalist economic systems including property rights, production, and exchange in developed nations (outline C.2).
Division of labour and role differentiation are explored as elements of economic systems across cultures (outline C.1.a).
Traces the evolution of economic systems from primitive to modern, including wealth distribution and property institutions (outline C).
Formal and informal education function as socialization mechanisms and cultural elements (outline D.1).
Explores how children are socialized across cultures and historical periods, relevant to enculturation (outline D.1).
Myth and folklore function as elements common to all cultures but differ in expression (outline D.2).
Mythological traditions serve as cultural elements that differ in expression between cultures (outline D.2).
Ritual is a major cultural element common to all societies, including rites of passage associated with kinship (outline D.2).
Recreation, sports, and games serve as cultural elements that differ across societies (outline D.6).
Musical expression as a cultural element that varies between cultures is directly relevant to artistic expression (outline D.4).
Addresses challenges to gender-based role differentiation and marriage customs, relevant to kinship and social change (outline A.6).
Confucian kinship systems, filial piety, and the family as a center for transmitting cultural values and power are central themes (outline A.5).
Group dynamics, in-group/out-group relations, and the varieties of associations within societies are key topics (outline B.1).
Explores how social identity is constructed through kinship, group membership, and cultural institutions (outline B.1).
Islamic family law, inheritance law, and legal systems function as cultural institutions shaping kinship and social structure (outline A.2 and D.3).
Examines the concept of home across cultures as a site of dwelling, belonging, and identity, relevant to A.1 (kinship and family) and C.1 (cultural components of societies).
